## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
        AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

will reformat to:
```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

Which will violate the max-line-length rule.

The rule may need to make an exception for parameterless functions, or honor the max line length.

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used:
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
```
[*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 120
```
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint 0.48.1, CLI

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 32→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting

 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39→ */
 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 44→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 45→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 46→                // violated
 47→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49→            ),
 50→        ),
 51→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 52→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 53→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 54→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 55→        ),
 56→    ) {
 57→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 62→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 63→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 64→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 65→        )
 66→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 75→            return
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 79→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 80→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 81→                node
 82→                    .children()
 83→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 89→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 90→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 91→            // skip lambda arguments
 92→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 93→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 94→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 95→        ) {
 96→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 97→            // - at least one of the arguments is
 98→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 99→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
100→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
101→        } else {
102→            false
103→        }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
108→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(lineIndent())
109→        return when {
110→            // IDEA quirk:
111→            // generic<
112→            //     T,
113→            //     R>(
114→            //     1,
115→            //     2
116→            // )
117→            // instead of
118→            // generic<
119→            //     T,
120→            //     R>(
121→            //         1,
122→            //         2
123→            //     )
124→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
125→
126→            // IDEA quirk:
127→            // foo
128→            //     .bar = Baz(
129→            //     1,
130→            //     2
131→            // )
132→            // instead of
133→            // foo
134→            //     .bar = Baz(
135→            //         1,
136→            //         2
137→            //     )
138→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
139→
140→            else -> currentIndentLevel
141→        }.let {
142→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
143→                it + 1
144→            } else {
145→                it
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
151→        newIndentLevel: Int,
152→        child: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
157→        when (child.elementType) {
158→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
159→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
160→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
161→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        prevLeaf.delete()
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            }
167→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
168→            ElementType.RPAR,
169→            -> {
170→                // aiming for
171→                // ... LPAR
172→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
173→                // <line indent> RPAR
174→                val intendedIndent = if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
175→                    indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
176→                } else {
177→                    indent
178→                }
179→
180→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
181→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
182→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
183→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
184→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
185→                        return
186→                    } else {
187→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
188→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
189→                        if (autoCorrect) {
190→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
191→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
192→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
193→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
194→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
195→                        }
196→                    }
197→                } else {
198→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
199→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
200→                    if (autoCorrect) {
201→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
202→                    }
203→                }
204→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
205→            }
206→        }
207→    }
208→
209→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
210→        when (node.elementType) {
211→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
212→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
213→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
214→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
215→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
216→        }
217→
218→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
219→        return children().any { child ->
220→            val elementType = child.elementType
221→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
222→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
223→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
224→        }
225→    }
226→
227→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
228→        treeParent.children()
229→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
230→            ?.children()
231→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
232→
233→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
234→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
235→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
238→        var prev = prevLeaf()
239→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
240→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
241→                return prev
242→            }
243→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
244→        }
245→        return null
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
249→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
250→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
251→        val controlFlowKeyword = when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
252→            is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
253→            is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
254→            is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
255→            else -> null
256→        } ?: return false
257→
258→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
259→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
260→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
261→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        }
263→        return true
264→    }
265→}

11→/**

14→public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE

18→
19→public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20→    EditorConfigProperty(
21→        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22→        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23→        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27→        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28→            when {
29→                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30→                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31→                }
32→
33→                /**
34→                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35→                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36→                 */
37→                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38→

40→                    PropertyType
41→                        .max_line_length
42→                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43→                        .let {

45→                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46→                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47→                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48→                                }
49→                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50→                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51→                                } else {
52→                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53→                                }
54→                            } else {
55→                                it.parsed
56→                            }
57→                        }
58→            }

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule` could violate the `max-line-length` rule by removing a newline before or after the colon in a function return type.

### Root Cause
The `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule` was designed to ensure a single space between the closing parenthesis and the colon, and between the colon and the return type. However, it did not take into account the `max_line_length` setting. When a function name or return type was very long, removing the newline to enforce a single space could lead to a line that exceeded the maximum allowed length.

### Changes Made
1.  Modified `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule` to use the `max_line_length` property from `.editorconfig`.
2.  Updated `removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon` to check if removing a newline before the colon would cause the resulting line to exceed `max_line_length`.
3.  Updated `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType` to check if replacing a newline after the colon with a space would cause the resulting line to exceed `max_line_length`.
4.  Implemented a helper method `lengthOfFirstLine` to accurately calculate the length of the text that would be joined when a newline is removed.

### Verification Results
I have added a new test file `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleMaxLineLengthTest.kt` with test cases that specifically target this issue.
-   A parameterless function with a long name and return type now correctly retains its newline if joining would exceed the limit.
-   Functions where joining does not exceed the limit are still reformatted to follow the standard spacing, ensuring no regression in existing functionality.
-   All new and existing tests passed successfully.

```kotlin
// Example of fixed behavior:
fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
    AndAlsoALongReturnType {
}
// This code will now remain as is if joining would exceed max_line_length.
```

 31→/**
 32→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 33→ *
 34→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39→ */
 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 44→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 45→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 46→                // violated
 47→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49→            ),
 50→        ),
 51→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 52→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 53→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 54→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 55→        ),
 56→    ) {
 57→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 62→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 63→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 64→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 65→        )
 66→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 75→            return
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 79→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 80→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 81→                node
 82→                    .children()
 83→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 89→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 90→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 91→            // skip lambda arguments
 92→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 93→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 94→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 95→        ) {
 96→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 97→            // - at least one of the arguments is
 98→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 99→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
100→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
101→        } else {
102→            false
103→        }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
108→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(lineIndent())
109→        return when {
110→            // IDEA quirk:
111→            // generic<
112→            //     T,
113→            //     R>(
114→            //     1,
115→            //     2
116→            // )
117→            // instead of
118→            // generic<
119→            //     T,
120→            //     R>(
121→            //         1,
122→            //         2
123→            //     )
124→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
125→
126→            // IDEA quirk:
127→            // foo
128→            //     .bar = Baz(
129→            //     1,
130→            //     2
131→            // )
132→            // instead of
133→            // foo
134→            //     .bar = Baz(
135→            //         1,
136→            //         2
137→            //     )
138→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
139→
140→            else -> currentIndentLevel
141→        }.let {
142→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
143→                it + 1
144→            } else {
145→                it
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
151→        newIndentLevel: Int,
152→        child: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
157→        when (child.elementType) {
158→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
159→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
160→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
161→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        prevLeaf.delete()
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            }
167→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
168→            ElementType.RPAR,
169→            -> {
170→                // aiming for
171→                // ... LPAR
172→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
173→                // <line indent> RPAR
174→                val intendedIndent = if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
175→                    indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
176→                } else {
177→                    indent
178→                }
179→
180→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
181→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
182→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
183→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
184→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
185→                        return
186→                    } else {
187→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
188→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
189→                        if (autoCorrect) {
190→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
191→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
192→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
193→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
194→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
195→                        }
196→                    }
197→                } else {
198→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
199→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
200→                    if (autoCorrect) {
201→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
202→                    }
203→                }
204→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
205→            }
206→        }
207→    }
208→
209→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
210→        when (node.elementType) {
211→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
212→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
213→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
214→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
215→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
216→        }
217→
218→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
219→        return children().any { child ->
220→            val elementType = child.elementType
221→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
222→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
223→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
224→        }
225→    }
226→
227→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
228→        treeParent.children()
229→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
230→            ?.children()
231→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
232→
233→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
234→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
235→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
238→        var prev = prevLeaf()
239→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
240→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
241→                return prev
242→            }
243→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
244→        }
245→        return null
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
249→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
250→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
251→        val controlFlowKeyword = when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
252→            is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
253→            is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
254→            is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
255→            else -> null
256→        } ?: return false
257→
258→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
259→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
260→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
261→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        }
263→        return true
264→    }
265→}
266→
267→public val ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ArgumentListWrappingRule().ruleId

 79→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted

372→ * assertions operate on the same state.

374→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
375→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */

379→    /**

381→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.

385→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId

387→    /**
388→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the

392→     */
393→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
394→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
395→
396→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
397→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
398→
399→        assertAll(
400→            {
401→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
402→                    .describedAs(
403→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
404→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
405→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
406→                            } else {
407→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
408→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
409→                            },
410→                    )
411→                    .isEmpty()
412→            },
413→            {
414→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
415→                    .describedAs(
416→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
417→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
418→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
419→                            } else {
420→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
421→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
422→                            },
423→                    )

673→        .map { ruleProvider ->
